name: Dou Hua
steps: Soak soy bean with water overnight (12hrs)
Drain next day, 100g soy bean (before soak) + 500ml filtered water
Blitz and squeeze the soy beans
Bring to boil, pass through muslin cloth. Cool down and keep it for the next day
Bring the soy milk to boiled then pass through muslin again
Cool the soy milk down to 82C (while waiting mix the gypsum powder and corn flour and still water in a bowl)
Mix the soy milk and the gypsum solution together and cover it with cling film. Let it set for 30min at room temperature.

notes: Can keep soy milk up to 4 days
Have soy milk ready, but make dou hua daily for freshness
